items.title: Valid certificates, stolen accounts: how attackers broke npm's last trust signal
items.summary: On May 19, 633 malicious npm package versions passed Sigstore provenance verification . They were cleared by the system because the attacker had generated valid signing certificates from a compromised maintainer account. Sigstore worked exactly as designed: it verified the package was built in a CI
